caveats.text: Builder self-audit falsely claimed no background tasks were used during build. Background tasks WERE used. Substantive PIT-safety was independently verified and accepted per the Stack Acceptance Memo (2026-04-15). Data integrity is confirmed but process attestation from the original builder is not trustworthy.

Rolling Statcast fields use available-only averaging. When a start within a rolling window lacks Statcast enrichment, pandas rolling().mean() silently averages over the non-null subset. A last_3 window with 1 unenriched start produces a value from 2 observations, indistinguishable from a fully observed window.

mlb_matchup_table_base is the real current base object that was referred to in prior prompts as 'mlb_canonical_historical_research_matchup_object'. That intended canonical name never existed as a separate artifact. This package formally resolves the naming: mlb_matchup_table_base IS the canonical object.

The intended governance layer (orchestration system, approved/banned manifests, branch status schema) does not exist and has never been created. This foundation package provides included/excluded artifact manifests as a practical substitute for formal manifests. It does NOT provide orchestration, routing, or workflow automation. Those remain missing.

Umpire ratings repair is documentation-only (3 doc files, no data parquet). The actual fix was applied directly to config.py UMPIRE_RATINGS. The repair docs are authoritative as a record of what was changed but there is no standalone data artifact to independently verify the repair.
